chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-816 of 2009 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Before; Mr. Justice Muhammad Shafi Siddiqui. Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah. 17.05.2022 Mr. Muhammad Arshad S. Pathan, Advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. == The petitioners by way of instant petition have sought for direction against the respondents from interfering with their possession over their houses situated in village “Beero Panhwar”. With this, they have also sought for compensation for burning their houses allegedly by the respondents. As per comments filed by the Additional Deputy Commissioner-I Hyderabad, no village with name of “Beero Panhwar” is available in Village Directory. If, it is believed that Village “Beero Panhwar” is actually available and petitioners are residing therein and they are going to be dispossessed therefrom by causing damage to their houses by the respondents then such dispute involving factual controversy could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its writ jurisdiction. Consequently, the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed accordingly together with the pending application, if any. J U D G E J U D G E Muhammad Danish*
